questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $A, B, C$ be $\mathrm{n} \times \mathrm{n}$ real matrices and are pairwise commutative and $\mathrm{ABC}=\mathrm{O}_{\mathrm{n}}$ and if $\lambda=\operatorname{det}\left(A^3+B^3+C^3\right) \cdot \operatorname{det}(A+B+C)$ then
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & A^3+B^3+C^3=A^3+B^3+C^3-3 A B C=(A+B+C)\left(A^2+B^2+C^2-A B-B C-C A\right) \\ & =(A+B+C)\left(A+\omega B+\omega^2 C\right)\left(A+\omega^2 B+\omega C\right) \quad\{\omega=\text { cube root of unity }\}\end{aligned}$
$=(A+B+C)\left(A+\omega B+\omega^2 C\right)\left(\overline{A+\omega B+\omega^2 C}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & =(A+B+C)\left(A+\omega B+\omega^2 C\right)\left(A+\omega B+\omega^2 C\right) \\ & \text { then } \operatorname{det}\left(A^3+B^3+C^3\right) \cdot \operatorname{det}(A+B+C)=\operatorname{det}(A+B+C)^2 \cdot \operatorname{det}\left(A+\omega B+\omega^2 C\right) \overline{\operatorname{det}\left(A+\omega B+\omega^2 C\right)} \\ & =(\operatorname{det}(A+B+C))^2 \cdot\left|\operatorname{det}\left(A+\omega B+\omega^2 C\right)\right|^2 \geq 0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the system of equation $2 x-y+z=0, x-2 y+z=0$ and $a x-y+2 z=0$ has infinitely many solutions and $f(x)$ be a continuous function such that $f(5+x)+f(x)=2 \forall x \in R$, then $\int_0^{-2 a} f(x) d x$ is equal to:
options:
$2 a$
[Note: $[k]$ denotes greatest integer less than or equal to $k$.]
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \Delta=0, \quad a=5 \\ & \int_0^{-10} f(x) d x=\int_0^{-5} f(x) d x+\int_{-5}^{-10} f(x) d x=\int_{-5}^{-10} f(x+5) d x+\int_{-5}^{-10} f(x) d x=2 \int_{-5}^{-10} d x=-10\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $\left|\begin{array}{ccc}2 a & 2 b & b-c \\ 2 b & 2 a & a+c \\ a+b & a+b & b\end{array}\right|$ is divisible by -
questions.solution:
$\Delta=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}2 \mathrm{a} & 2 \mathrm{~b} & \mathrm{~b}-\mathrm{c} \\ 2 \mathrm{~b} & 2 \mathrm{a} & \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{c} \\ \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b} & \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b} & \mathrm{b}\end{array}\right|$
$\begin{aligned} & R_2 \rightarrow R_2+R_1 \\ & \Delta=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}2 a & 2 b & b-c \\ 2(a+b) & 2(a+b) & a+b \\ a+b & a+b & b\end{array}\right| \\ & \Delta=(a+b)\left|\begin{array}{ccc}2 a & 2 b & b-c \\ 2 & 2 & 1 \\ a+b & a+b & b\end{array}\right| \\ & C_1 \rightarrow C_1-C_2 \\ & =(a+b)\left|\begin{array}{ccc}2(a-b) & 2 b & b-c \\ 0 & 2 & 1 \\ 0 & a+b & b\end{array}\right| \\ & =(a+b) \cdot 2(a-b)(2 b-a-b) \\ & = \\ & =2(a+b)(a-b)(b-a)=-2(a+b)(a-b)^2 .\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\left|\begin{array}{ccc}x^2-5 x+3 & 2 x-5 & 3 \\ 3 x^2+x+4 & 6 x+1 & 9 \\ 7 x^2-6 x+9 & 14 x-6 & 21\end{array}\right|=a x^3+b x^2+c x+d$ then which of the following are correct?
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}x^2-5 x+3 & 2 x-5 & 3 \\ 3 x^2+x+4 & 6 x+1 & 9 \\ 7 x^2-6 x+9 & 14 x-6 & 21\end{array}\right|$
$f^{\prime}(x)=0$
$\Rightarrow f(x)$ is a constant polynomial and $f(0) \neq 0$
$\Rightarrow d \neq 0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}2 \sin x & \sin ^2 x & 0 \\ 1 & 2 \sin x & \sin ^2 x \\ 0 & 1 & 2 \sin x\end{array}\right|$, then
options: Tangent to the curve $y=f(x)$ at $x=0$ is $y=0$
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}2 \sin x & \sin ^2 x & 0 \\ 1 & 2 \sin x & \sin ^2 x \\ 0 & 1 & 2 \sin x\end{array}\right|=2 \sin x\left(4 \sin ^2 x-\sin ^2 x\right)-\sin ^2 x(2 \sin x)=6 \sin ^3 x-2 \sin ^3 x$
$\begin{aligned} & f(x)=4 \sin ^3 x \\ & \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(x)=12 \sin ^2 x \cos x\end{aligned}$
(B) $f^{\prime}(\pi / 2)=12 \sin ^2(\pi / 2) \cos (\pi / 2)=0$
(C) $f(-x)=-f(x)$ odd function
$\therefore \quad \int_{\frac{-\pi}{2}}^{\frac{\pi}{2}} f(x) d x=0$
(D) at $x=0, y=0$
$\Rightarrow\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)_{(0,0)}=0$ tangent at $(0,0)$
$\begin{aligned} & y-0=\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)_{(0,0)}(x-0) \\ & \Rightarrow y=0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
System of equation
$\begin{aligned} & x+3 y+2 z=6 \\ & x+\lambda y+2 z=7 \\ & x+3 y+2 z=\mu \text { has }\end{aligned}$
questions.solution:
$x+3 y+2 z=6$....(i)
$x+\lambda y+2 z=7$....(ii)
$x+3 y+2 z=\mu$....(iii)
(A) If $\lambda=2$, then $D=0$, therefore unique solution is not possible
(B) If $\lambda=4, \mu=6$
$\begin{aligned} & x+3 y=6-2 z \\ & x+4 y=7-2 z\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad y=1$ and $x=3-2 z$
Substituting in equation (iii)
$3-2 z+3+2 z=6$ is satisfied
$\therefore \quad$ infinite solutions
(C) $\lambda=5, \mu=7$
consider equation (ii) and (iii)
$\begin{aligned} & x+5 y=7-2 z \\ & x+3 y=7-2 z\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad y=0, x=7-2 z$ are solution
sub. in (i)
$7-2 z+2 z=6$ does not satisfy
$\therefore \quad$ no solution
(D) if $\lambda=3, \mu=5$
then equation (i) and (ii) have no solution
$\therefore \quad$ no solution
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $x$ is real and $\Delta(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}x^2+x & 2 x-1 & x+3 \\ 3 x+1 & x^2+2 & x^3-3 \\ x-3 & x^2+4 & 2 x\end{array}\right|=a_0 x^7+a_1 x^6+a_2 x^5+\ldots \ldots+a_6 x+a_7$, then:
questions.solution:
(1)
$$
a_7=\Delta(0)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}
0 & -1 & 3 \\
1 & 2 & -3 \\
-3 & 4 & 0
\end{array}\right|=21
$$
(2) and (4) $\Delta(1)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}2 & 1 & 4 \\ 4 & 3 & -2 \\ -2 & 5 & 2\end{array}\right|=132=\sum_{k=0}^7 a_k$
Now, $\quad \sum_{k=0}^6 a_k=\sum_{k=0}^7 a_k-a_7=132-21=111$
(3) $\Delta(-1)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}0 & -3 & 2 \\ -2 & 3 & -4 \\ -4 & 5 & -2\end{array}\right|=-32$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
